TASK.TITLE: Frontend-Backend Authorization & Contract Consistency Audit
TASK.DESCRIPTION: Perform a comprehensive audit to ensure that frontend authorization logic and backend authorization rules are aligned across the system. The goal is to detect and prevent mismatches like role-based access discrepancies between frontend guards and backend permission checks.

OBJECTIVES.OBJECTIVE: Identify all role-based access rules implemented in frontend
Identify corresponding authorization rules in backend
Verify that roles and permissions are consistent between FE and BE
Detect any mismatches (e.g. AUTHOR-only vs AUTHOR+EDITOR)
Ensure no frontend guard is stricter or looser than backend policy

CHECKS.ITEM: Role checks used in UI rendering
Role validation logic (AUTHOR, EDITOR, USER, etc.)

REQUIREMENTS.ITEM: Compare frontend guards with backend authorization rules for each protected feature
List all detected mismatches and categorize them (frontend stricter, backend stricter, or inconsistent)
Provide clear recommendation for alignment (frontend → backend or backend → frontend)
Ensure analysis is based on actual code behavior, not assumptions

REPORT_FILE.NAME: auth-consistency-report.txt
REPORT_FILE.DESCRIPTION: Human-readable report summarizing: - Current frontend authorization rules - Current backend authorization rules - Detected mismatches - Root causes - Recommended fixes

OPTIONAL_STRUCTURED_REPORT.NAME: auth-consistency.xml
OPTIONAL_STRUCTURED_REPORT.DESCRIPTION: Structured XML report listing all mismatches between FE and BE authorization logic.

CONSTRAINTS.ITEM: No code changes required unless explicitly fixing detected mismatches
Focus on analysis and detection rather than implementation
Use actual codebase evidence to justify findings
Do not assume roles or permissions that are not present in the code
